steps.title: Spot the defining phrase
steps.content: The passage calls these Wikipedia entries "fictitious" — made-up entries for nonexistent people like The Deadweights and Théophile Figeys. They persisted "before they were finally recognized as ___ and deleted." The blank names what the fictitious entries were recognized as. It must mean invented falsehoods.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean invented or made-up things. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — enhancements: means improvements — recognizing fake entries as "improvements" makes no sense; they were harmful hoaxes, not improvements to Wikipedia. ✗
  • Choice B — analyses: means examinations or evaluations — a fictitious entry for a fake band is not an analysis of anything. ✗
  • Choice C — fabrications: means invented falsehoods — exactly what fictitious entries are: completely made-up facts presented as real. ✓
  • Choice D — revelations: means disclosed truths or surprising discoveries — the opposite of a fabrication; revelations expose truth, whereas these entries hid falsehoods. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
